_generated: GENERATED by harness-kit from plugin.meta.toml / harness.toml — do not edit
name: question-walkthrough
description: Adaptive one-question-at-a-time decision walkthrough. Takes a pile of open questions or undecided to-dos — mined from the conversation, read from a pointed-at document, given inline, or pulled from task systems — confirms the pile, sequences it by leverage (answers that could moot or reshape other questions go first), then walks it one AskUserQuestion at a time. Every non-obvious question is fronted by a pre-read — why it exists, impact, trade-offs, terms — delivered in a chat turn that ends BEFORE the dialog is raised (same-turn context is invisible context). Answer notes are classified and honored: option modifiers apply immediately; directives — extra work or a redirect that replaces the answer — get a one-line repeat-back and confirm, then run in an end-of-walk batch by default. After every answer the remaining pile is re-planned: mooted questions dropped loudly, order revised, implied follow-ups added with consent. Decisions are recorded back at their source and summarized in a final outcome table with parked items and directive outcomes called out. Pure-markdown skill, portable across Claude Code and Codex.
